questions.prompt: Which of the following are red flags for someone possibly suffering from depression?

questions.prompt: Which of the following aspects encompasses effective communication with a patient? Choose all that apply.

questions.prompt: Which of the following immunizations are routinely required for children? Choose all that apply.

Decreased blood pressure of the lower leg

questions.prompt: A patient is determined to have a rearfoot varus postural presentation. What are the chronic injuries that may occur because of this foot alignment?

Deferring all questions to the physician

questions.prompt: Which scenario would be an example of a breach of professional ethics? Choose all that apply.
choices: Consulting with the supervising physician for a new sign or symptom of concern
Leaving a computer or laptop screen open to a patient's chart unattended while performing manual intervention on a patient
Discussing a patient’s positive progress with another patient
Sharing patient information with the coach when the athlete asked for information to be kept confidential

questions.prompt: Which of the following factors may alter joint arthrokinematics?

questions.prompt: Between which two articulations does rotation of the cervical spine occur?
choices: A combination of the occipitus, axis, and atlas

questions.prompt: A 16-year-old rugby player falls directly onto the ball during a tackle and immediately struggles to breathe, describing a sensation of being “winded.” They appear panicked but have normal chest rise and no visible signs of significant trauma. After a few minutes, their breathing begins to normalize, and they can speak clearly again. What is the most likely diagnosis?

choices: The patient has waived their right to confidentiality when there is a peer review.
The patient's identifiers must be removed before the peer review.
The peer reviewers must hold all information derived from the medical case confidential and sign a confidentiality agreement.

choices: Lowering the acidity of gastric acid produced

Biological in origin and cause a more intense inflammatory reaction
Made from polymers and do not cause an inflammatory reaction

Best for thicker tissue, used in callused skin, two-step stitch, increased ischemia if too tight

You notice an athlete who is having a harder time breathing during practice than normal. They are complaining of fatigue that is worse the longer they practice, and you think they look paler than normal. Upon assessing the athlete, you also notice their gums look pale. What condition does this athlete most likely have?

Which nutrient is the body's primary energy source?

choices: Can return to sport while taking the Valium
Avoid all rotations of the body or head

Verbal, nonverbal, implicit, or explicit discriminatory behavior such as sexism or racism

questions.title: Testlet 1
questions.scenario:
Please use the following information to answer questions in this testlet.
A men’s basketball player comes into the clinic holding his hand and lower arm. He is in obvious pain, with blood dripping off his fingertips, and his forearm has a 45° deformity.

questions.prompt: Using the SAMPLE format for obtaining a history, what questions should be asked? Choose all that apply.
choices: How long ago did this happen?
Can you describe the pain to me?

questions.prompt: Using the SAMPLE format for obtaining a history, what additional questions should be asked? Choose all that apply.
choices: Who was with you at the time of the accident?
How long have you been experiencing this problem?
Are there any past illnesses, operations, or injuries that pertain to this injury?

questions.prompt: Read the athlete information. Your observation reveals the forearm is most likely fractured. The finger appears to have been completely avulsed. You need to find the avulsion so it can be surgically reattached. Which of the following is the best way to handle the recovery of the avulsed piece? Choose all that apply
questions.supporting_text: He was one-arm dunking a basketball, and his forearm hit the rim and broke. On the way out of the rim, his ring got stuck on the hook that holds the net to the rim. The rim broke off the glass backboard, and glass scattered on the floor.
choices: Use sterile saline solution to clean the avulsion of glass.
Use sterile gauze soaked in sterile saline solution to care for the avulsion and place in a plastic bag.
Use water to clean the avulsion of glass shards.
Place the avulsion in a sterile plastic bag after it is wrapped in gauze.
Place the avulsion on ice once in the plastic bag.
Place the avulsion in ice water once in the plastic bag.

questions.prompt: The avulsed portion of the finger has been collected. What is the immediate care for the avulsed finger? Choose all that apply.
choices: Cover with a dry sterile dressing.
Cover with a wet sterile dressing.
Use personal protection precautions to protect yourself from blood.
Apply a constricting band proximal to the injury.

questions.prompt: The bleeding for the finger is now controlled, and the avulsed tissue is awaiting transportation to the hospital. You must now provide care for the deformity of the forearm. You have determined that both the radius and ulna have been fractured. What are the steps, in order, for caring of the forearm fracture? Drag the list items into the correct order.

tokens: Assess the pulse, movement, and sensation.
Stabilize the joints above and below the fractures.

zones.accept: Assess the pulse, movement, and sensation.
Stabilize the joints above and below the fractures.

questions.title: Testlet 2
questions.scenario:
Please use the following information to answer the questions in this testlet.
A patient reports blunt force trauma to the abdomen from a baseball bat. He indicates he is nauseated.

questions.prompt: When observing the patient, the athletic trainer checks to determine the extent of the injury. The athletic trainer needs to determine if the patient is experiencing a hemorrhage. Match each type of hemorrhage with the indications.

pairs.left: Coughing up blood from the lungs

questions.prompt: When observing the patient, the athletic trainer checks to determine the extent of the injury. The athletic trainer needs to determine if the patient is experiencing a hemorrhage. Match each additional type of hemorrhage with the indications.

pairs.left: Larger areas of mucosal or skin hemorrhage

questions.prompt: When palpating the abdominal quadrants, what types of signs and symptoms is the athletic trainer assessing? Choose all that apply.
When performing percussion testing on the abdomen, what is the proper order? Place the number in the circle on the abdomen, with 1 being the first percussion and 13 being the last.

image.alt: Anterior view of the abdomen with thirteen marked percussion sites.

questions.prompt: At this point, the athletic trainer decides to take the patient’s blood pressure. What are the contraindications to taking blood pressure in a particular extremity? Choose all that apply.
Click on the area of the extremity where the head of the stethoscope should be placed while taking blood pressure.

hs.alt: Front view of a shirtless man standing with one arm slightly away from the body and the palm facing forward, used for hotspot placement on the upper extremity.

questions.prompt: Match the Korotkoff sounds with the phase of assessing blood pressure.
The patient has a blood pressure of 96/60 and a pulse rate of 20, and he is increasingly agitated. What injuries or illnesses should be suspected at this time? Choose all that apply.

questions.title: Testlet 3
questions.scenario:
Please use the following information to answer the questions in this testlet.
A middle-distance runner on the track team comes into the athletic training facility complaining of pain in their left lower leg. The pain has been getting progressively worse, but today the pain has caused him to stop his workout. The athlete is currently participating in the indoor portion of the season.

questions.prompt: What questions does the athletic trainer need to ask regarding the history of this injured athlete? Choose all that apply.
choices: Have you ever injured this leg previously?
What makes the injured leg feel better?
What makes the leg feel worse?
Does your pain radiate up or down the leg?
On a scale of 0 to 10, how would you rate your pain?
How is your significant other doing?

questions.prompt: Read the patient information. The athletic trainer tries to isolate the location of the pain. What structures would the athletic trainer palpate related to this injury? Choose all that apply. (Palpations are negative and pain-free unless otherwise noted.)
questions.supporting_text:
The information shared by the patient is as follows:
     -The leg hurts during workouts and has gotten worse over the last few weeks.
     -Today is the worst pain and is 7 on a 10-point pain scale.
     -Rest calms the pain, but it takes longer to calm down.
     -Running causes the pain, and the more they run, the worse is the pain.
     -(+) numbness and tingling on the top of the foot
choices: Extensor digitorum longus (painful along the length of the muscle)
Extensor hallucis longus (painful along the length of the muscle)

questions.prompt: Read the patient information. The athletic trainer tries to isolate the location of the pain. What structures would the athletic trainer palpate related to this injury? Choose all that apply. (Palpations are negative and pain-free unless otherwise noted.)
questions.supporting_text:
The information shared by the patient is as follows:
     -The leg hurts during workouts and has gotten worse over the last few weeks.
     -Today is the worst pain and is 7 on a 10-point pain scale.
     -Rest calms the pain, but it takes longer to calm down.
     -Running causes the pain, and the more they run, the worse is the pain.
     -(+) numbness and tingling on the top of the foot
choices: Dorsalis pedis artery (diminished on the left side compared to the right)
Posterior tibial artery (diminished on the left side compared to the right)
Anterior tibialis muscle and tendon (painful along the length of the muscle)

questions.prompt: Read the additional information. Identify the compartments of the lower leg from the drawing.
questions.supporting_text:
The following information has now been found:
     -(+) Diminished pulse in the left dorsalis pedis
     -(+) Pain along the extensor digitorum longus
     -(+) Pain along the extensor hallucis longus
     -(+) Diminished pulse in the posterior tibial artery
     -(+) Pain along the anterior tibialis muscle

image.alt: Lower leg compartment diagram labeled a through d.

questions.prompt: The athletic trainer does a range of motion assessment. Which motions should be assessed? Choose all that apply. AROM
choices: Dorsiflexion (difficult to perform fully, is painful, reduced approximately 15°)

questions.prompt: The athletic trainer does a range of motion assessment. Which motions should be assessed? Choose all that apply. PROM
choices: Plantar flexion (passive overpressure causes significant pain)

questions.prompt: A strength assessment is performed next. The results are shown. Choose all that apply. RROM
choices: Dorsiflexion (The patient tested 3-/5 (fair-) upon a manual muscle test.)
Plantar flexion (The patient tested 4/5 (good) upon a manual muscle test.)
Inversion (The patient tested 4/5 (good) upon a manual muscle test.)
Eversion (The patient tested 4/5 (good) upon a manual muscle test.)

questions.prompt: What is the athletic trainer’s differential diagnosis of this athlete’s injury? Please identify those injuries or conditions that you believe are a result of your examination. Choose all that apply.
questions.supporting_text: The following information has been provided: AROM -Dorsiflexion (Passive overpressure gains an additional 5°.) -Plantar flexion (Passive overpressure causes significant pain.) -Inversion (Passive overpressure gains an additional 3°.) -Eversion (Passive overpressure gains an additional 4°.) PROM -Dorsiflexion (The patient tested 3-/5; fair-.) -Plantar flexion (The patient tested 4/5; good.) -Inversion (The patient tested 4/5; good.) -Eversion (The patient tested 4/5; good.)

questions.title: Testlet 4
questions.scenario:
Please use the following information to answer the questions in this testlet.
A cross-country runner has a lower-leg stress fracture. The physician has asked that the athletic trainer maintain the cardiovascular integrity of this patient while healing occurs.

questions.prompt: What are the most common sites of stress fracture in the lower extremity? Choose all that apply
The athletic trainer decides to use aquatic rehabilitation for this patient. Which of the following are contraindications to aquatic exercise? Choose all that apply.

The athletic trainer decides to place the athlete in the pool for aquatic exercise. What are the upper and lower target heart rate zones for this individual if he is submerged up to his neck? Choose all that apply.
Match each of the following exercises and activities to the appropriate level of rehabilitation (advanced, intermediate, or initial). Check your answers when you have finished.

pairs.right: Total land-based program; pool program: deep water with no flotation equipment
Sport-specific activities in the water, using flotation cuffs

questions.prompt: Which of the following photos shows appropriate taping techniques for shin splints when this athlete is ready to return to competition? Choose all that apply.

questions.title: Testlet 5
questions.scenario:
Please use the following information to answer the questions in this testlet.
A patient has recently had hand surgery, and the incision is not healing as quickly as the physician would like. The patient is in no pain. The patient’s incision is on the volar surface of the hand, midway between the metacarpal heads and the wrist.

questions.prompt: The athletic trainer must determine which therapeutic modalities can aid in the healing process. Choose all that apply.
When using high-voltage stimulation, which waveform diagram corresponds to which type of therapy? Match the waveform diagram to the electrotherapy it best describes.

The patient’s incision, indicated by the black line, is on the volar surface of the hand, midway between the metacarpal heads and the wrist. Click on the area where the active high voltage stimulation (HVS) electrode pad should be on this patient.

hs.alt: Front view of a shirtless man standing with one arm slightly away from the body and the palm facing forward, used for hotspot placement on the upper extremity.

questions.prompt: Click on the area where the dispersive high-voltage stimulator (HVS) electrode pad should be placed on this patient.

hs.alt: Front view of a shirtless man standing with one arm slightly away from the body and the palm facing forward, used for hotspot placement on the upper extremity.

questions.prompt: Why would the athletic trainer use a high-voltage stimulator (HVS) in this instance? Choose all that apply.
choices: It decreases the granulation of tissue.

questions.title: Testlet 6
questions.scenario:
Please use the following information to answer the questions in this testlet.
A patient is in rehabilitation for an injury to the lateral collateral ligament tear of the elbow. The patient is a tennis player and indicates that the injury began when she was training too much about a year ago.

questions.prompt: What are the tests that specifically test for lateral epicondylitis? Choose all that apply.
What muscles are most likely to be involved in lateral epicondylitis? Choose all that apply.
Which improper tennis technique is likely to contribute to lateral epicondylitis? Choose all that apply.
choices: The wrist flexed during the backhand
A forehand drop shot using a scooping motion
A backhand where the elbow leads ahead of the racquet
Overhead shots with the wrist in pronation and hyperextension

questions.prompt: Conservative treatment has not helped this patient, and the physician determines that surgical intervention has the best chance for returning the patient to her previous level of competitiveness. What is the primary focus of immediate postoperative management for lateral epicondylitis surgery? Choose all that apply.
choices: Range of motion allowed from 45° to 120°

questions.prompt: Match the phase of rehabilitation with the predominant tissue healing phase.

Read the patient information.
The athletic trainer is suspicious that there is an underlying issue that may be a cause for referral to the physician. Given the signs and symptoms, what would the athletic trainer suspect is occurring? Choose all that apply.
questions.supporting_text:
A week into the rehabilitation process, the patient begins to have the following signs and symptoms:
     -(+) Increased pain that is disproportionate to the injury
     -(+) Pain even with light touch
     -(+) Decreased range of motion
     -(+) Redness of upper extremity
     -(+) Excessive sweating
     -(+) Decreased distal pulses

questions.prompt: What are some of the treatments that the athletic trainer can use for treating a complex regional pain syndrome? Choose all that apply.
choices: Guided exercise to increase range of motion

questions.prompt: The athlete returns from the physician, and the athletic trainer is instructed to apply a TENS unit to relieve pain. Click on the areas where the two electrodes should be placed on the cutaneous nerves.

hs.alt: Front view of a shirtless man standing with one arm slightly away from the body and the palm facing forward, used for hotspot placement on the upper extremity.

questions.prompt: As the patient is progressing through their treatment, a part of the process is to reduce oedema, and massage has been selected. Match the type of massage technique and its use.

pairs.left: Relaxes patient, increases surface circulation, and distributes lubrication
Increases circulation, promotes venous and lymphatic return, increases elasticity of skin, breaks up adhesions
Aids absorption of oedema, reduces inflammation, reduces muscular spasm

questions.title: Testlet 7
questions.scenario:
Please use the following information to answer the questions in this testlet.
A football player was injured in a game the previous night. He indicates he took a helmet to the anterior superior iliac spine of the hip. X-rays of the affected hip and pelvis are negative.

questions.prompt: This patient has an antalgic gait. What signs would the athletic trainer likely see when evaluating this patient’s gait? Choose all that apply.
choices: Body weight is shifted over the unaffected hip.
The velocity of walking and cadence are decreased.
Patient’s affected-side foot is plantar flexed.
Stance phase on the affected leg is shorter.
Swing phase of the unaffected leg is shorter.

questions.prompt: As part of the assessment process, the athletic trainer needs to determine the anterior-posterior pelvic tilt on the affected side. Select all the measurements that the athletic trainer must take.

Read the information obtained during the assessment.
The athletic trainer decides to use ice and ultrasound over the area. The athletic trainer wants to use direct contact ultrasound. Which of the following is the protocol for direct coupling ultrasound? Choose all that apply.
questions.supporting_text:
The following is information obtained during the assessment of this injury:
      -(+) Pain over ASIS
      -(+) Discoloration over ASIS
      -(+) Decreased active ROM with hip flexion
      -(+) Decreased active ROM with trunk flexion
      -(+) Pain with trunk rotation
      -(+) Anterior pelvic tilt on the affected side
choices: Apply coupling gel to the treatment area.
Determine the treatment intensity based on the tissue depth.
Verify the output intensity is 0 before turning it on.
Determine the duration of treatment based on the size of the area.
Apply coupling gel to the ultrasound head.
Maintain contact in a circular or linear pattern.

questions.prompt: The athletic trainer needs to develop a plan for this patient to strengthen the area. Match each muscle with the exercise used for strengthening.

pairs.left: Gluteus medius, gluteus maximus, and tensor fasciae latae
Erector spinae multifidus, gluteals, and obliques

questions.prompt: The patient is ready to return to full-contact play, and the athletic trainer needs to create a protective thermoplastic pad to prevent reinjury. Place the layers in the order in which they would be applied to the hip, starting with the bottom layer. Drag the correct layer name into each numbered slot.

questions.title: Testlet 8
questions.scenario:
Please use the following information to answer the questions in this testlet.
A patient has suffered a wrist fracture. Post-treatment, the fracture was immobilized for six weeks, and the patient now has stiffness in the wrist.

questions.prompt: Look at the photo of the acute injury and identify the type of fracture that has most likely occurred. Choose only one.
Which statements describe the mechanism of injury for this fracture? Choose all that apply.
choices: A fall onto an outstretched hand
Wrist in neutral with a direct blow to the forearm
Wrist pronated with a direct blow to the forearm
Wrist supinated with a direct blow to the forearm

questions.prompt: Once the athletic trainer has received clearance from the physician, they decide to use joint mobilization to address the stiffness. This patient has no contraindication to joint mobilization. Match each joint to the correct loose-packed position.

pairs.left: Distal radioulnar anteroposterior (AP) and posteroanterior (PA) glides

pairs.right: Neutral wrist with slight ulnar deviation
Neutral wrist flexion, slight ulnar deviation, and neutral forearm supination

questions.prompt: Click on the areas to indicate the placement of the athletic trainer’s hands on this patient to perform wrist traction.

hs.alt: Close-up of a hand and wrist held in a claw-like position, used for hotspot placement of hand positions during wrist traction.

questions.prompt: The athletic trainer elects to perform wrist ventral (volar) glide mobilization on this patient. Select the photo that best depicts the proper ventral (volar) glide technique. Select only one.
The athletic trainer elects to perform wrist radial glide mobilization on this patient. Select the photo that best depicts the proper wrist dorsal glide technique. Select only one.

questions.title: Testlet 9
questions.scenario:
Please use the following information to answer the questions in this testlet.
A patient with hip and abdominal weakness has been given a home exercise program to strengthen various muscle groups.

questions.prompt: The patient needs both open- and closed-chain activities. Select the exercises that represent open-chain activities around the hip and abdomen. Choose all that apply.
The patient has been performing planks. As you observe her planks, what area(s) need correction for proper form? Choose all that apply.
choices: Improve head position to align with the trunk.
Distribute equal pressure on elbows and toes.
Keep the feet at a 45° angle.

questions.prompt: Which major muscle groups are activated in the following exercises? Match each exercise to the muscle group(s) used.

questions.title: Testlet 10
questions.scenario:
Please use the following information to answer the questions in this testlet.
The athletic trainer has been tasked with creating a portion of the standing orders. The specific segment assigned is medical testing options.

questions.prompt: For athletes with a soft-tissue organ injury, what medical test options should be recommended? Choose all that apply.
Which of the following tests should be recommended for suspected vascular concern? Choose all that apply.
Review the photo. Is it an X-ray, CT scan, or bone scan?
Review the photo. Is it an X-ray, CT scan, or bone scan?
Review the photo. Is it an X-ray, CT scan, or bone scan?
Review the photo. Is it an X-ray, CT scan, or bone scan?
What is the concept that helps to explain how bone develops its shape from forces that are exerted upon it?
When you stretch a muscle, if you release the stretch force and the muscle returns to its original length, which property is the muscle performing?
To avoid fracture, which of the following is the proper hand position stressed during the application of cardiopulmonary resuscitation?
Which of the following statements is accurate about a graded exercise test?
choices: With ECG, it is the first choice for evaluating myocardial ischemia in individuals with a normal resting ECG and the ability to exercise.
It is considered an invasive procedure.
It should be performed in individuals with an intermediate risk of CAD.
If a lower rate–pressure product occurs during the onset of ischemia, this is always indicative of disease progression.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool to monitor how hard the patient perceives he is working during a GXT?
Which of the following is not a site where enterally delivered drugs are typically absorbed?
Which is not a site where the majority of drugs are absorbed in the body?
Which of the following statements is true?
choices: Blood pressure is the product of cardiac output and stroke volume.
Systolic pressure normally decreases in response to increasing exercise intensities because of a reduction in the total peripheral resistance.
Systolic pressure that increases more than 10 mmHg in response to an increased workload is an indication for terminating a graded exercise test.
An exaggerated blood pressure response during a graded exercise test in a normotensive person indicates an increased risk for developing hypertension.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the 6 min walk test an important indicator?
What is the primary purpose of universal precautions?
choices: Reduce the spread of HIV from Africa to the United States
Reduce the risk of contracting an infectious disease
Reduce the spread of tuberculosis among urban dwellers
Reduce the spread of HIV from mother to child

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a mode of transmission for HIV?
Which group has the highest likelihood of suffering a spinal cord injury?
Which of the following are typical symptoms of someone suffering from an acute stroke?
choices: Numbness or weakness of the face, arm, or leg
Confusion, speech problems, and cognitive defects

questions.prompt: Why are children at a greater risk for heat-related illness?
choices: They have higher subcutaneous fat stores.
They have a reduced capacity to evaporate sweat.

questions.prompt: What is the respiratory rate during sub-maximum exercise of children compared to adults?
choices: Marked by a higher rate and shallow breathing response
Marked by a deeper breathing response

questions.prompt: Which of the following do resistance training prescriptions for children not use?
Apart from the physical benefits, what are the important potential benefits of a community-based exercise program?
Which of the following is considered the “gold standard” assessment for depression?
choices: Clinical interview by a trained mental health professional
Asking the patient if he is depressed

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a symptom of anxiety?
Which of the following is a chromosomal condition caused by the presence of all or part of an extra 21st chromosome?
In patients, which of the following conditions are skeletal muscle hypotonia and joint laxity often present, which is why it is important to avoid hyperextension and hyperflexion?
What is the type of leadership that involves the simple exchange of one thing for another in a relationship between two people?
